steps.title: Identify roots from the graph
steps.content: Reading the graph, the x-intercepts occur at $x = -5$, $x = 0$, and $x = 4$. This immediately eliminates choices C and D, which have roots at $x = -4$ and $x = 5$ instead.

steps.title: Check end behavior to determine degree
steps.content: Both ends of the graph point downward, so the polynomial must have even degree and a negative leading coefficient. Choice A is cubic, so it would have opposite end behavior. Choice B is quartic because of $(x+5)^2$, so it fits.

steps.title: Confirm root multiplicity at x = −5
steps.content: At $x=-5$, the graph touches the x-axis without crossing, which means the root has even multiplicity. Choice B has the factor $(x+5)^2$, so it matches the intercepts and the bounce. That makes B correct.
